ddls:
CREATE TABLE "statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic entry */,
  "ref_date" INTEGER /* Reference date for the statistic (year) */,
  "geo" TEXT /* Geographical region where the statistic is applicable */,
  "dguid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the dataset */,
  "statistics_type" TEXT /* Type of statistic being measured */,
  "uom" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the statistic */,
  "uom_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the unit of measurement */,
  "scalar_factor" TEXT /* Scalar factor for the measurement */,
  "scalar_id" INTEGER /* Scalar ID related to the statistic */,
  "vector" TEXT /* Vector reference for multi-dimensional data */,
  "coordinate" TEXT /* Coordinate reference for geographical data */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the statistic */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Status of the statistic entry (e.g., valid, invalid) */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol representing the statistic type */,
  "terminated" TEXT /* Indicates if the entry has been terminated */,
  "decimals" INTEGER /* Number of decimal places for the statistic value */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the statistic entry */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the entry */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the entry */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the entry */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the statistic entry */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., survey, census, administrative records) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_uom_id FOREIGN KEY ("uom_id") REFERENCES units_of_measurement ("uom_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_geo FOREIGN KEY ("geo") REFERENCES regions ("region_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES statistics_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_scalar_id FOREIGN KEY ("scalar_id") REFERENCES scalar_factors ("scalar_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographical region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the region was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the region */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the region */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units_of_measurement" (
  "uom_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the unit of measurement */,
  "uom_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit of measurement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement unit */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the unit of measurement was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the unit of measurement */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the unit of measurement */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the unit of measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("uom_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_units_of_measurement_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_units_of_measurement_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scalar_factors" (
  "scalar_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the scalar factor */,
  "scalar_name" TEXT /* Name of the scalar factor */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the scalar factor */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the scalar factor was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the scalar factor */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the scalar factor */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the scalar factor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scalar_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scalar_factors_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scalar_factors_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistics_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the status was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the status */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the status */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_status_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_status_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., statistician, data analyst, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the user */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last login of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
